```python
import pytest

def clamp(value: float, min_val: float, max_val: float) -> float:
    return max(min_val, min(value, max_val))

# Happy path tests: Normal cases where clamping works as expected
@pytest.mark.parametrize("value, min_val, max_val, expected", [
    (5.0, 0.0, 10.0, 5.0),  # Value within range
    (-5.0, 0.0, 10.0, 0.0),  # Value below min
    (15.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0),  # Value above max
    (0, 0, 10, 0),  # Integer inputs (should work as floats)
    (10, 0, 10, 10),
])
def test_clamp_happy_path(value, min_val, max_val, expected):
    assert clamp(value, min_val, max_val) == expected

# Edge cases: Boundary values and special conditions
@pytest.mark.parametrize("value, min_val, max_val, expected", [
    (0.0, 0.0, 10.0, 0.0),  # Value equals min
    (10.0, 0.0, 10.0, 10.0),  # Value equals max
    (5.0, 5.0, 5.0, 5.0),  # Min and max are equal, value equals them
    (3.0, 5.0, 5.0, 5.0),  # Min and max equal, value below
    (7.0, 5.0, 5.0, 5.0),  # Min and max equal, value above
    (5.0, 10.0, 0.0, 10.0),  # Min > max (function still clamps to min)
])
def test_clamp_edge_cases(value, min_val, max_val, expected):
    assert clamp(value, min_val, max_val) == expected

# Error cases: Invalid inputs that should raise exceptions
@pytest.mark.parametrize("value, min_val, max_val", [
    ("string", 0.0, 10.0),  # Non-numeric value
    (5.0, "string", 10.0),  # Non-numeric min_val
    (5.0, 0.0, "string"),  # Non-numeric max_val
    (None, 0.0, 10.0),  # None value
    (5.0, None, 10.0),  # None min_val
    (5.0, 0.0, None),  # None max_val
])
def test_clamp_error_cases(value, min_val, max_val):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        clamp(value, min_val, max_val)
```
